title: Algorithms in Python
description: Sorting, searching, recursion, dynamic programming with Python.

lessons.title: Linear Search
lessons.subtitle: Find by scanning
lessons.starterCode:
def find(nums, target):
    # TODO: return the index of target, or -1 if missing
    pass

print(find([3, 7, 1, 9, 4], 9))
lessons.task: Implement linear search. Return the index of target in nums, or -1 if not found. The call find([3,7,1,9,4], 9) must print 3.
lessons.hint: Iterate with enumerate() and return the index when a match is found.

lessons.title: Binary Search
lessons.subtitle: Log(n) lookup
lessons.starterCode:
def bsearch(nums, target):
    # TODO: return index of target in sorted nums, or -1
    pass

print(bsearch([1, 3, 5, 7, 9, 11], 7))
lessons.task: Implement binary search on a sorted list. Must print 3.
lessons.hint: Maintain lo and hi bounds; check nums[mid] each iteration.

lessons.title: Reverse a List
lessons.subtitle: In-place mutation
lessons.starterCode:
nums = [1, 2, 3, 4, 5]
# TODO: reverse nums in place (don't use reversed())
print(nums)
lessons.task: Reverse the list in place using index swaps. Expected [5, 4, 3, 2, 1].
lessons.hint: Swap nums[i] and nums[len(nums)-1-i] for i up to len(nums)//2.

print(fib(20))
lessons.hint: Base cases: fib(0)=0, fib(1)=1. Cache results in memo.

lessons.title: Two Sum
lessons.subtitle: Hash map lookups
lessons.starterCode:

print(two_sum([2, 7, 11, 15], 9))
lessons.task: Return the two indices whose values sum to the target. Must print (0, 1).
lessons.hint: Store value→index in a dict; check for target-value as you iterate.

lessons.title: Merge Sort
lessons.subtitle: Divide & conquer

def merge_sort(nums):

    pass

print(merge_sort([3, 1, 4, 1, 5, 9, 2, 6]))
lessons.task: Implement merge sort recursively. Must print [1, 1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, 9].
lessons.hint: Split, sort each half, then merge with two pointers.

lessons.title: Longest Substring
